label: Calendar Events
description: Economic calendar and corporate actions feed. Shows upcoming events with expected impact ratings.

0.6.note: Loading handled inside CalendarEventFeed via useEconomicResults/useCorporateActions isLoading guards.

0.7.note: 'No events in range' and 'No corporate actions in range' empty states in CalendarEventFeed.

0.8.note: Error boundary in widget-wrapper covers render errors; CalendarEventFeed sub-hooks surface loading state.

0.9.note: No dead code. Widget is a minimal 12-line wrapper.

l0.notes: All L0 checks pass. Widget delegates all logic to CalendarEventFeed component.

1.1.note: Data sourced from useEconomicResults and useCorporateActions hooks via CalendarEventFeed — no inline fetch in widget.

1.2.note: Calendar hooks use useAuth() for user-scoped queries.

1.3.note: Calendar data is global macro/corporate-action data — not org-filtered.

1.6.note: No scope filtering; hook fetches all events in time window.

l1.notes: L1 pass. 1.3/1.4/1.5/1.6 N/A — global macro data not filtered by trading scope.

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab and f9f29ea. Re-audit only if wiring test fails.

3.1.note: Modes: live ✓ (events fetched via API), batch ✓ (calendar data is time-bound; events for the asOf date are still relevant), paper N/A.

3.2.note: Calendar data is not mode-sensitive — economic events are static scheduled events regardless of live/batch mode.

3.5.note: Paper mode: no behavioral change — calendar events are read-only reference data.

3.6.note: No WebSocket or timed intervals in widget file. CalendarEventFeed uses REST hooks only.

l3.notes: Agent checks pass. Human checks 3.3(N/A — read-only widget)/3.4(N/A — no mode-sensitive display) required but expected to be N/A.

4.1.note: No buttons in widget file — all interactivity inside CalendarEventFeed.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks in widget render path.

l4.notes: All agent checks pass. Human checks 4.7(N/A)/4.8(scroll within div.h-full.overflow-auto) required.

5.1.note: Target strategies: all — economic calendar is reference data shared across all strategy types. Not strategy-specific.

l6.notes: L1.5 harness spec: 11 tests covering tab render (Macro/Corporate/Market), loading state, today-filter (isTodayUtc), event label + importance badge, count badges, past-event exclusion. See tests/widgets/terminal/calendar-events.test.tsx

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.7.note: No hooks in widget file. CLEAN.

l7.notes: L7 pass. Minimal wrapper — nothing to fix.

8.1.note: No text-[…] in widget file. CLEAN.

8.3.note: Widget file has no custom divs that should be primitives — single wrapper div is intentional layout container. CalendarEventFeed is a trading component.

8.4.note: Agent: no img, input, interactive div in widget file. Human: keyboard + a11y check on CalendarEventFeed required.

8.5.note: Human check required — responsive at minW=3, minH=2. Available on terminal and overview tabs.

keepSeparate.rationale: Calendar events is global macro reference data — not driven by instrument selection. It is standalone even though it sits on the terminal tab.

relationships.notes: Standalone widget — not coupled to terminal-watchlist selection. Available on terminal and overview tabs.

dataContract.notes: Data fetched via useEconomicResults() and useCorporateActions() hooks in CalendarEventFeed. No real-time streaming required — economic calendar events are scheduled and change infrequently.

gaps: DeFi governance events / protocol upgrades not in calendar data
Corporate actions only relevant for TradFi/CeFi equities
coverage.notes: Calendar data coverage depends on the backend data provider. DeFi-specific events (protocol upgrades, token unlocks) are a gap in current data model.

findings.description: calendar-events-widget.tsx — no user-visible strings in widget file itself; all strings in CalendarEventFeed component
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21
